hero.title: Fallout: New Vegas Remaster Wiki
hero.description: Return to the Mojave Wasteland and shape its future through faction alliances, recruitable companions, branching quests and multiple endings. Develop unique character builds, face the challenges of Hardcore Mode and decide who will control New Vegas.

falloutBeginnerGuide.title: Fallout New Vegas Remaster Beginner Guide
falloutBeginnerGuide.intro: Every Courier's journey starts in Goodsprings. Learn how SPECIAL attributes, skills, perks, reputation, karma, and survival systems connect, and follow a clear early route that helps you avoid the most common wasteland mistakes.

steps.title: Build Your Courier with SPECIAL
steps.description: SPECIAL (Strength, Perception, Endurance, Charisma, Intelligence, Agility, Luck) defines everything your character can do. Pick attributes that match your playstyle, then choose two Tag Skills and up to two Traits during character creation in Goodsprings.

steps.title: Level Up Skills Every Level
steps.description: Each level grants skill points to spend on combat, stealth, science, repair, speech, and survival skills. Skill checks gate dialogue options, hacking, lockpicking, and crafting, so a balanced spread opens more of the Mojave than a narrow one.

steps.title: Pick the Right Perks Every Two Levels
steps.description: Perks are earned every two levels and each has skill or level requirements. Strong early perks like Educated, Comprehension, and Swift Learner accelerate your long-term power, while combat perks like Commando or Sniper define your fighting style.

steps.title: Track Reputation and Karma
steps.description: Reputation measures how each faction views you, while Karma tracks your overall morality. Quests, theft, and key story decisions shift both, and they directly affect vendor prices, companion reactions, and which endings remain available.

steps.title: Survive Hardcore Mode (Optional)
steps.description: Hardcore Mode adds dehydration, starvation, sleep deprivation, ammo weight, and slower healing. It rewards the Survival skill, cooking, and careful resource planning, and is required for the Hard Core achievement.

steps.title: Set Your Early Priorities
steps.description: A reliable early route follows the main quest from Goodsprings through Primm, Nipton, Novac, and Boulder City to reach the New Vegas Strip. Along the way, gather caps, recruit a companion, and upgrade your gear before committing to a faction.

quickTips: Use VATS in early fights to land reliable hits and study enemy weak points.
Save often and rotate multiple save slots before dialogue and faction decisions.
Repair weapons and armor at vendors to keep their damage and damage threshold high.
Invest in Speech and Lockpick early to unlock extra dialogue options, loot, and quest routes.
Collect and sell early loot in Goodsprings and Primm to afford your first real weapon upgrades.

falloutQuestsWalkthrough.title: Fallout New Vegas Remaster Quests Walkthrough
falloutQuestsWalkthrough.intro: The Mojave is built around branching choices. This walkthrough organizes the main quest, the four major faction arcs, every companion storyline, and the most important side quests, with objectives, skill checks, missable rewards, and clearly marked spoilers.

faqs.question: What is the main quest path to New Vegas?
faqs.answer: The main quest starts in Goodsprings and runs through Primm, Nipton, Novac, and Boulder City before you reach the New Vegas Strip. Once inside, Ring-a-Ding-Ding! reunites you with Benny, after which the story branches into the four faction endgame arcs.

faqs.question: How do the NCR faction quests work?
faqs.answer: The New California Republic questline begins through Ambassador Crocker or Colonel Moore. The capstone mission For the Republic, for Democracy tasks you with securing Hoover Dam and eliminating rival factions, and your reputation with the NCR determines available missions and rewards.

faqs.question: What does the Caesar's Legion questline offer?
faqs.answer: Caesar's Legion is joined through Vulpes Inculta or Lucius at The Fort. Render Unto Caesar sends you on a series of conquests east and west of the Colorado, ending with Veni, Vidi, Vici at Hoover Dam for a totalitarian Mojave.

faqs.question: How do I side with Mr. House?
faqs.answer: Mr. House is contacted after meeting him in the Lucky 38. The House Always Wins chain asks you to neutralize the Brotherhood of Steel and secure the Securitron army, culminating in You'll Know It When It Happens and the defense of the dam.

faqs.question: What is the Yes Man independent route?
faqs.answer: Yes Man unlocks the Wild Card chain, letting the Courier seize control of New Vegas without any of the major factions. You upgrade the Securitron army, neutralize rival leaders, and finish with No Gods, No Masters for an independent Mojave.

faqs.question: Which companion quests should I not miss?
faqs.answer: Every permanent companion has a personal quest triggered by travelling with them: Boone's One for My Baby, Cassidy's Heartache by the Number, Raul's Old School Ghoul, Veronica's I Could Make You Care, Arcade's For Auld Lang Syne, Lily's quiet triggers, ED-E My Love, and Rex's Nothin' But a Hound Dog.

faqs.question: What are the most rewarding side quests?
faqs.answer: Beyond the Beef at the Ultra-Luxe, Come Fly With Me at REPCONN Test Site, The Legend of the Star for Sunset Sarsaparilla caps, and the Goodsprings and Novac defense quests are all high-value side quests with strong loot, caps, and reputation rewards.

falloutBuilds.title: Fallout New Vegas Remaster Builds
falloutBuilds.intro: Builds in the Mojave are driven by SPECIAL allocation, Tag Skills, Traits, and perk order. These recommended builds cover every major combat style plus a Hardcore survival specialist, each with the attributes and progression that make them work.

builds.name: Guns Sniper
builds.description: Long-range stealth sniper built around Sneak, Guns, and crit perks like Better Criticals and Sniper. The Anti-Materiel Rifle and All-American scale hard with headshots from stealth.

builds.name: Energy Weapons
builds.description: Plasma and laser powerhouse using the Q-35 Matter Modulator, Plasma Caster, and Pew-Pew. Tesla armor and Laser Commander push damage output to endgame levels.

builds.name: Melee Bruiser
builds.description: Close-quarters juggernaut with Super Sledge, Oh, Baby!, and Slayer. High Strength and Endurance feed damage, health, and the Stonewall and Unstoppable perks.

builds.name: Unarmed Brawler
builds.description: Ballistic Fist and Pushy build with Piercing Strike and Slayer for rapid knockdowns. Ninja and Unstoppable add stealth and crowd-control synergy.

builds.name: Explosives Demolitionist
builds.description: Grenade Machine Gun, Mercy, and Annabelle with Demolition Expert and Heave, Ho!. Great against clustered enemies and Deathclaw packs.

builds.name: Stealth and Speech Courier
builds.description: A pacifist-friendly build stacking Speech, Sneak, and Barter to talk and sneak through almost every quest, ideal for non-lethal and roleplay runs.

builds.name: Hardcore Survivor
builds.description: Built for Hardcore Mode with high Survival for cooking, Lead Belly, and Chem Resistant. Turns food, water, and campfire recipes into a self-sustaining progression engine.

builds.name: Jack-of-All-Trades
builds.description: A flexible generalist using Educated and Comprehension to keep most skills viable, perfect for a first playthrough that wants to sample every quest and weapon type.

falloutWeaponsAndGear.title: Fallout New Vegas Remaster Weapons and Gear
falloutWeaponsAndGear.intro: Compare the strongest weapons, armor, ammunition, and unique equipment in the Mojave by damage, damage type, requirements, and where to find them. Use these recommendations to plan loadouts for early, mid, and late game.

rows.name: Anti-Materiel Rifle
rows.notes: .50 MG sniper rifle, the strongest long-range option; buy from Gun Runners or NCR armories.

rows.name: Brush Gun
rows.notes: .45-70 lever-action rifle with high per-shot damage; great mid-to-late general weapon.

rows.name: Plasma Caster
rows.notes: Heavy plasma weapon with high DPS; pairs with Optimized and Max Charge ammo.

rows.name: Oh, Baby!
rows.notes: Unique Super Sledge found in Charleston Cave on Jacobstown mountain.

rows.name: Ballistic Fist
rows.notes: Shotgun-gauntlet for unarmed builds; looted from Legionaries and Caesar's tent.

rows.name: Grenade Machine Gun
rows.notes: Area-clearing splash weapon; sold by Gun Runners and Quartermaster Bardon.

rows.name: T-51b Power Armor
rows.notes: Top-tier power armor with high Damage Threshold; requires Power Armor Training.

rows.name: Ranger Combat Armor
rows.notes: Iconic NCR Veteran Ranger armor; great balance of protection without training.

rows.name: .308 JSP Ammo
rows.notes: Hand-loaded ammo for sniper rifles; boosts damage at the cost of faster degradation.

rows.name: Stealth Boy
rows.notes: Consumable that grants invisibility; essential for stealth approaches and ambushes.

falloutCompanions.title: Fallout New Vegas Remaster Companions
falloutCompanions.intro: Up to two companions can travel with you at once, and each one brings a unique perk, combat style, and personal storyline. This guide covers every permanent companion, where to recruit them, and the choices that can permanently change their fate.

cards.name: Craig Boone
cards.description: A grieving NCR sniper recruited in Novac after solving One for My Baby. His Spotter perk tags enemies in VATS, and his quest unlocks one of three distinct endings.

cards.name: Rose of Sharon Cassidy
cards.description: Hardened caravan owner recruited at the Mojave Outpost after clearing her debt. Her Whiskey Rose perk boosts Damage Threshold and removes alcohol's negative effects.

cards.name: Raul Tejava
cards.description: A ghoul gunslinger freed from Black Mountain. His Regular Maintenance and Full Maintenance perks slow weapon and armor degradation, making him invaluable for long expeditions.

cards.name: Veronica Santangelo
cards.description: Recruited at the 188 Trading Post. Her Scribe Assistant perk grants access to workbenches in the field, and her quest I Could Make You Care reshapes her ties to the Brotherhood.

cards.name: Arcade Gannon
cards.description: An idealistic Followers doctor recruited at the Old Mormon Fort. His Medical Informant perk boosts healing, and his quest For Auld Lang Syne ties directly into an Enclave endgame thread.

cards.name: Lily Bowen
cards.description: A gentle nightkin grandmother found in Jacobstown. Her Stealth Girl perk boosts Stealth Boys and melee sneak attacks, and her medication choices permanently affect her dialogue.

cards.name: ED-E
cards.description: A reactivated Enclave Eyebot recruited in Primm. Its sensors and combat buffs upgrade through ED-E My Love, and the chosen faction for the upgrade changes its final perk.

cards.name: Rex
cards.description: The King's loyal cyberdog in Freeside, available after Nothin' But a Hound Dog. Transplanting a new brain changes his combat stats and quest-related Search and Rescue perk.

falloutFactions.title: Fallout New Vegas Remaster Factions
falloutFactions.intro: Who controls New Vegas is the central question of the Mojave. Understand the four major factions and the minor powers, how reputation works, what each alliance demands, and which decisions lock you out of rival endings.

cards.name: New California Republic (NCR)
cards.description: A sprawling democratic republic striving to restore order and claim Hoover Dam's power. Allied through Ambassador Crocker or Colonel Moore, the NCR values law, taxes, and military expansion across the Mojave.

cards.name: Caesar's Legion
cards.description: A brutal slave army modeling itself on Rome, massing east of the Colorado River. Joined through The Fort, the Legion rejects technology and democracy in favor of conquest and absolute order.

cards.name: Mr. House
cards.description: The reclusive techno-autocrat who rules the New Vegas Strip through his Securitron army. Siding with House means preserving his vision of an independent, ordered Vegas under his singular control.

cards.name: Yes Man (Independent)
cards.description: A reprogrammed Securitron that lets the Courier seize New Vegas personally. The Wild Card chain frees you from every major faction and ends with an independent Mojave ruled by your decisions.

cards.name: Great Khans
cards.description: A nomadic warrior tribe based at Red Rock Canyon, allied with the Legion by circumstance. Your actions can redirect their fate across several endings tied to their drug trade and history.

cards.name: Brotherhood of Steel
cards.description: A remnant chapter hidden in the Hidden Valley bunker, hoarding pre-war technology. Their strict codes and isolation make them a reluctant ally or a target depending on your faction path.

cards.name: Followers of the Apocalypse
cards.description: A humanitarian research group providing medicine and education from the Old Mormon Fort. They hold no military power but quietly shape the Mojave through knowledge and Arcade Gannon.

cards.name: Boomers
cards.description: Artillery-obsessed isolationists at Nellis Air Force Base. Earning their trust through Volare! grants access to howitzer support and their aid in the final battle for the dam.

falloutMods.title: Fallout New Vegas Remaster Mods
falloutMods.intro: Modding keeps the Mojave stable, beautiful, and endlessly replayable. These recommendations cover the essential stability fixes, performance and interface upgrades, visual overhauls, gameplay expansions, and the load-order basics that protect your saves.

cards.name: New Vegas Script Extender (NVSE)
cards.description: The foundation that most New Vegas mods depend on. Install it first, as countless gameplay and UI mods require the extra script functions NVSE provides.

cards.name: New Vegas Anti-Crash (NVAC)
cards.description: A memory and engine patch that prevents the most common random crashes. Pair it with the 4GB large-address-aware patch for smoother long sessions.

cards.name: YUP - Unofficial Patch
cards.description: A community-maintained bug-fix patch that corrects thousands of quest, item, and gameplay errors without changing the intended balance or content.

cards.name: User Interface Mods
cards.description: Mods like Darnified UI, MTUI, and the Mod Configuration Menu clean up fonts, lists, and settings access, making menus readable and mod options reachable.

cards.name: Texture and Lighting Mods
cards.description: NMC's Texture Pack, Poco Bueno, and ENB or ReShade presets dramatically upgrade terrain, buildings, and lighting while keeping the Mojave's desaturated wasteland tone.

cards.name: Project Nevada
cards.description: A modular overhaul adding sprint, bullet time, visors, grenades, and rebalanced implants. Each module can be toggled to match how much you want to change the base game.

cards.name: Content and Quest Mods
cards.description: Expansions like The Someguy Series, New Vegas Bounties, and New California add fully voiced quests, companions, and map areas that extend the Mojave far beyond the base game.

cards.name: Mod Organizer 2
cards.description: A profile-based mod manager that keeps your load order and installs isolated per profile, letting you test setups safely without overwriting your clean save files.

falloutLocations.title: Fallout New Vegas Remaster Locations
falloutLocations.intro: The Mojave is dense with settlements, vaults, military sites, casinos, and hidden landmarks. This regional guide highlights the quests, vendors, unique loot, skill books, and fast-travel points waiting at each major location.

cards.name: New Vegas Strip
cards.description: The glittering heart of the Mojave, home to the Tops, Gomorrah, and the Ultra-Luxe casinos, the Lucky 38, and Mr. House. Entry requires a pass, 2,000 caps, or a high Science check.

cards.name: Goodsprings
cards.description: Your starting settlement and tutorial hub. Doc Mitchell, Chet's store, and the Goodsprings Source are here, and defending the town in Ghost Town Gunfight sets your early reputation tone.

cards.name: Hoover Dam
cards.description: The strategic prize of the Mojave that powers New Vegas and the NCR. The dam hosts the final battle for every faction's endgame, plus heavy weapons and armor from Quartermaster Bardon.

cards.name: Vault 22
cards.description: An overgrown vault overrun by mutated spore plants and carriers. Keely's research and the quest Bleed Me Dry lead here, and the flame weapons inside counter its spore threats.

cards.name: HELIOS One
cards.description: A pre-war solar power plant controlled by the NCR. That Lucky Old Sun lets you redirect its power and decide the fate of the ARCHIMEMES weapon test.

cards.name: The Fort
cards.description: Caesar's fortified camp across the Colorado River, reachable by Cottonwood Cove. It houses the weathered Securitron vault and Caesar's tent, central to the Legion questline.

cards.name: Jacobstown
cards.description: A mountain resort refuge for super mutants led by Marcus. It ties into Lily's recruitment, the Charleston Cave unique melee weapon, and the Nightkin cure quest.

cards.name: Black Mountain
cards.description: A broadcasting stronghold held by Tabitha's super mutants and nightkin. Clearing it recruits Raul Tejada and unlocks the strongest unique weapon options nearby.

faq.title: Frequently Asked
faq.subtitle: Everything you need to know about Fallout New Vegas Remaster

questions.question: What is Fallout New Vegas Remaster?
questions.answer: Fallout New Vegas Remaster is the refined modern edition of Obsidian's 2010 post-apocalyptic RPG, returning players to the Mojave Wasteland with upgraded visuals and the full quest, faction, companion, and modding depth of the original.

questions.question: Which factions can you join in Fallout New Vegas Remaster?
questions.answer: Fallout New Vegas Remaster offers four major factions: the NCR, Caesar's Legion, Mr. House, and the Yes Man independent route. Each faction has its own questline and a distinct ending decided at Hoover Dam.

questions.question: Does Fallout New Vegas Remaster include Hardcore Mode?
questions.answer: Yes. Fallout New Vegas Remaster keeps Hardcore Mode, adding dehydration, starvation, sleep, and ammo weight for players who want a more demanding survival experience and its dedicated achievement.

questions.question: How many companions are in Fallout New Vegas Remaster?
questions.answer: Fallout New Vegas Remaster has eight permanent companions, including Boone, Cassidy, Raul, Veronica, Arcade, Lily, ED-E, and Rex, each with recruitment requirements, a personal quest, and a unique perk.

questions.question: Are mods compatible with Fallout New Vegas Remaster?
questions.answer: Yes. Fallout New Vegas Remaster supports the established modding ecosystem, with stability fixes, UI upgrades, visual overhauls, and gameplay mods installed through NVSE and Mod Organizer 2.

questions.question: Where can I get Fallout New Vegas Remaster?
questions.answer: Fallout New Vegas Remaster is available through Steam and official Bethesda channels, with the official add-ons Dead Money, Honest Hearts, Old World Blues, and Lonesome Road included in the Ultimate edition.
